title: boebera porophyllum
intro: Boebera porophyllum, also known as Porophyllum ruderale, is a plant species native to the Americas, commonly referred to as "Mexican tea" or "Mexican oregano." It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a popular choice for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Boebera porophyllum include its ability to alleviate digestive problems, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Boebera porophyllum can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, or used in a variety of other herbal preparations, including tinctures, ointments, and topical creams.
